calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Regina Delaney, 760-218-3898 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Lawrence Sussman, Esq. 415-788-7000 Defendant in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LAWRENCE SUSSMAN's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

Defendant Lawrence Sussman's demurrer to the sole cause of action for fraud in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Regina Delaney is sustained without leave to amend. The second amended complaint, read in conjunction with the judicially noticeable documents, discloses on its face that it is barred by the three year statute of limitations for fraud claims. Ms. Delaney failed to plead facts showing that the discovery rule prevents her claim from being time-barred, nor is it reasonably possible that she could do so. The second amended complaint together with the documents which the court judicially notice in CGC-05-440065 and BASF Case No. 04-044) show that no later than 2005 Ms. Delaney was on inquiry notice about the facts underlying her allegations against Mr. Sussman and that a reasonable investigation at that time would have revealed them. (E-Fab, Inc. v. Accountants, Inc. Services (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1308, 1319 ("[f]or purposes of accrual of the limitations period, inquiry notice is triggered by suspicion. . . .Once the plaintiff has a suspicion of wrongdoing, and therefore an incentive to sue, she must decide whether to file suit or sit on her rights")). At all times since 2005 the court records in the partition action were available for Ms. Delaney to inspect and her failure to inspect them is not grounds to toll or defer the statute of limitations. Because of her suspicion about the alleged inadequacy of Mr. Sussman's representation and his alleged repeated refusal to produce documents she requested, Ms. Delaney was on inquiry notice no later than 2005 that Mr. Sussman may not have been honest or fair with her. Ms. Delaney was also on notice as of 2005 about her contentions that she was not treated fairly or properly regarding the sale price of the property, the alleged mismanagement of the escrowed sales proceeds, and Mr. Sussman's alleged substandard representation of her and his alleged lack of expertise regarding real property.

Order and Judgment signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration And/Or Ccp 473 Relief
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Regina Delaney (760-218-3898) appears In Propria Persona as self-represented plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

PLAINTIFF REGINA DELANEY'S Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration And/Or Ccp 473 Relief is GRANTED. The court vacates the February 14, 2018 order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend and the subsequent judgment. Plaintiff did not serve the instant motion in a timely fashion, but defendant waived the defect by addressing the merits. (See Carlton v. Quint, (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697.) Plaintiff, who is acting in pro per, demonstrates mistake, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect. Plaintiff mistakenly believed that her second amended complaint would be filed and the demurrer hearing would be mooted. The court notes that there is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Plaintiff has leave to file the second amended complaint by March 29, 2018, and defendant may demur to that pleading if he desires. Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.com (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lawrence Sussman, Esq. 415-788-7000 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LAWRENCE SUSSMAN DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

Defendant Lawrence Sussman's demurrer to the first through sixth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Regina Delaney is sustained without leave to amend as to all six causes of action. No opposition filed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) A proposed judgment is submitted to the Court for review.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)
